sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 매출 1,815억 1,900만 달러와 순이익 302억 5,500만 달러를 기록했지만, 금리·무역정책 변동과 AI 투자 확대가 분기 후반 리스크를 좌우할 수 있다.

keyPoints.text: 매출 1,815억 1,900만 달러, 영업이익 238억 5,200만 달러, 순이익 302억 5,500만 달러로 분기 손익 체력은 유지됐다.
Three Months Ended March 31 기준 Consolidated 매출은 155,667에서 181,519로 늘어 'Sales increased 17% in Q1 2026 compared to the comparable prior year period'로 정리되며 성장의 방향은 명확하다.
현금흐름 측면에서는 영업활동 현금흐름 26,032와 투자활동 (64,212)가 동시에 보고되어, 분기 운영은 영업생성 현금과 투자 집행이 함께 큰 폭으로 움직인 형태다.

riskChanges.text: 추가 자금조달을 위해 추가 주식이나 전환사채를 발행하면 기존 주주 지분 희석이 발생할 수 있고, 인수·투자 확대가 유동성 필요성을 키워 다시 자금조달 수요를 유발할 수 있다는 점이 위험으로 제시됐다.
추가 신용라인 또는 기타 금융수단을 우리가 원하는 규모·조건으로 받을 수 있다는 보장이 없고, 정책금리 및 자본시장 변동성이 미래 차입비용을 올릴 수 있어 조달비용 리스크가 상존한다.
인플레이션·금리·자원/공급 변동성, 예측하기 어려운 글로벌 tariff 및 무역 정책 변화, AI 기술의 확대가 수요, 비용, 신기술 편익의 예측을 어렵게 만들어 Q2 2026까지 영향이 지속될 수 있다는 점을 경고했다.

businessChanges.text: 변동환율 영향으로 Q1 2026 net sales가 $2.9 billion 늘었으므로, 순성장 판단 시 환율 기여분과 실제 판매·수요 요인을 분리해 볼 필요가 있다.
자금의 주원천으로 영업현금과 현금·현금성자산·시장성 유가증권이 제시됐고, 공정가치 기준 잔액이 123.0 billion에서 143.1 billion로 상승해 유동성 버팀목 자체는 확대된 것으로 보인다.

financialHighlights.text: 매출 1,815억 1,900만 달러, 영업이익 238억 5,200만 달러, 순이익 302억 5,500만 달러가 당분기 성적표로 공개됐다.
총자산은 9,166억 3,000만 달러, 현금및현금성자산은 1,018억 1,600만 달러로 기재돼 분기 말 자본구조 기반을 확인할 수 있다.

watchItems.text: 영업활동 26,032와 투자활동 (64,212)의 차이가 큰 구조에서 추가 신용라인 가용성이 제한될 경우, 향후 AI 투자 집행 속도가 곧 유동성 관리 성패의 핵심 지표가 될 수 있다.
매출은 'Sales increased 17%'였고 환율 효과가 $2.9 billion 반영된 만큼, 글로벌 tariff 및 무역 정책 변화가 더해지면 분기 성장 품질을 계속 추적하는 것이 필요하다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2026-03-31 revenue = 1,815억 1,900만 달러 (USD 181.519B; 181,519 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 operating income = 238억 5,200만 달러 (USD 23.852B; 23,852 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 net income = 302억 5,500만 달러 (USD 30.255B; 30,255 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 total assets = 9,166억 3,000만 달러 (USD 916.63B; 916,630 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 cash and cash equivalents = 1,018억 1,600만 달러 (USD 101.816B; 101,816 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31.

Item 1A of Part II, “Risk Factors.” We continually evaluate opportunities to sell additional equity or debt securities, obtain credit facilities, obtain finance and operating lease arrangements, enter into financing obligations, repurchase common stock, pay dividends, repurchase, refinance, or otherwise restructure our debt, or access capital through other financing arrangements for strategic reasons or to further strengthen our financial position. The sale of additional equity or convertible debt securities would be dilutive to our shareholders. In addition, we will, from time to time, consider the acquisition of, or investment in, complementary businesses, products, services, capital infrastructure, and technologies, which might affect our liquidity requirements or cause us to secure additional financing, or issue additional equity or debt securities. There can be no assurance that add

itional credit lines or financing instruments will be available in amounts or on terms acceptable to us, if at all. In addition, economic conditions and actions by policymaking bodies are contributing to changing interest rates and significant capital market volatility, which, along with any increases in our borrowing levels, could increase our future borrowing costs. 27 Table of Contents Results of Operations We have organized our operations into three segments: North America, International, and AWS. These segments reflect the way the Company evaluates its business performance and manages its operations. See Item 1 of Part I, “Financial Statements — Note 8 — Segment Information.” Overview Macroeconomic factors, including changes in inflation and interest rates, resource and supply volatility, global economic and geopolitical developments, including unpredictable shifts in global tariff

and trade policies, and the development and adoption of technologies and services, including artificial intelligence, have direct and indirect impacts on our results of operations that are difficult to predict, isolate, and quantify. These could affect customer demand for our products and services, our ability to forecast growth needs, expenses, and benefits from new technologies. Further, we expect to continue making additional investments in our artificial intelligence initiatives. We expect some or all of these factors to continue to impact our results of operations into Q2 2026. Net Sales Net sales include product and service sales. Product sales represent revenue from the sale of products and related shipping fees and digital media content where we record revenue gross. Service sales primarily represent third-party seller fees, which includes commissions and any related fulfillment

and shipping fees, AWS sales, advertising services, Amazon Prime membership fees, and certain digital media content subscriptions. Net sales information is as follows (in millions): Three Months Ended March 31, 2025 2026 Net Sales: North America $ 92,887 $ 104,143 International 33,513 39,789 AWS 29,267 37,587 Consolidated $ 155,667 $ 181,519 Year-over-year Percentage Growth: North America 8 % 12 % International 5 19 AWS 17 28 Consolidated 9 17 Year-over-year Percentage Growth, excluding the effect of foreign exchange rates: North America 8 % 12 % International 8 11 AWS 17 28 Consolidated 10 15 Net Sales Mix: North America 60 % 57 % International 21 22 AWS 19 21 Consolidated 100 % 100 % Sales increased 17% in Q1 2026 compared to the comparable prior year period. Changes in foreign exchange rates increased net sales by $2.9 billion for Q1 2026. For a discussion of the effect of foreign exc

Liquidity and Capital Resources Cash flow information is as follows (in millions): Three Months Ended March 31, Twelve Months Ended March 31, 2025 2026 2025 2026 Cash provided by (used in): Operating activities $ 17,015 $ 26,032 $ 113,903 $ 148,531 Investing activities (29,803) (64,212) (106,283) (176,954) Financing activities (47) 52,767 (10,603) 62,475 Our principal sources of liquidity are cash flows generated from operations and our cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities balances, which, at fair value, were $123.0 billion and $143.1 billion as of December 31, 2025 and March 31, 2026. Amounts held in foreign currencies were $29.7 billion and $22.1 billion as of December 31, 2025 and March 31, 2026. Our foreign currency balances include British Pounds, Canadian Dollars, Euros, Indian Rupees, and Japanese Yen. Cash provided by (used in) operating activities was $17.0 billion
